source_jobs.title: Mission Software Engineer - Honolulu, HI
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ABOUT THE ROLE:

As a Mission Software Engineer at HavocAI, you’ll be at the heart of delivering autonomy where it matters most — in the field, alongside our customers, solving real-world challenges. This isn’t just about writing code; it’s about building, deploying, and owning software that ensures our autonomous systems perform flawlessly in operational environments.

You’ll work across networking, autonomy, systems integration, and robotics to create mission-ready solutions that directly impact national security and maritime operations. From developing new capabilities to troubleshooting live deployments, you’ll balance speed, scalability, and engineering excellence with a relentless mission-first mindset.

If you thrive in dynamic environments, enjoy collaborating with multidisciplinary teams, and want to see your work tested and proven in the real world, this role offers the chance to make an immediate and meaningful impact.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES AND REQUIREMENTS:

 - Own and Deliver Solutions: Take ownership of software solutions deployed to customers, ensuring high-quality delivery that meets mission objectives.

 - Improve Mission Capabilities: Develop software that enhances HavocAI products for specific operational needs, collaborating with the platform engineering team to scale capabilities to broader use cases.

 - Collaborate Across Teams: Work closely with multidisciplinary teams to plan, build, and test complex functionality.

 - Analyze and Monitor: Create and leverage performance metrics for debugging and monitoring deployed systems.

 - Troubleshoot and Resolve: Identify root causes of system issues, propose solutions, and coordinate next steps.

 - Partner with End-Users: Collaborate with customers and end-users to translate needs into features while balancing user experience with technical constraints.

 - Real-World Deployment




QUALIFICATIONS:

 - Strong engineering background, ideally in fields such as Computer Science, Software Engineering, Mathematics, or Physics.

 - 3+ years of experience with C++, Python, or Rust in a Linux development environment.

 - Experience building software solutions that involve significant data processing and analysis.

 - Ability to navigate and understand complex systems and established codebases quickly.

 - Passion for working on mission-critical software with real-world impact.

 - Must be a United States citizen and eligible for US Clearance.





PREFERRED SKILLS:

 - Experience developing and testing multi-agent autonomous systems deployed in real-world scenarios.

 - Familiarity with robotics and autonomy frameworks such as ROS or MOOS-IvP.

 - Experience working with flight systems, safety-critical software, or hardware integration.

 - Understanding of distributed communication networks and messaging standards.

 - Knowledge of military systems and operational tactics.




LOCATION:

 - Must be based in the Honolulu, HI area or willing to relocate.

 - Onsite presence is required.


BENEFITS:

 - 100% Employer paid Health, Dental and Vision Insurance for you and your families

 - Life Insurance (Employer Paid)

 - Ability to participate in the companies 401k program (Matching)

 - Unlimited PTO policy with an enforced 2 week minimum

 - Equity Package

 - Work / Home Office Stipend

 - Global Entry

 - 16 Week Paid Parental Leave

 - Monthly

source_jobs.title: Sr. Mechanical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ABOUT THE ROLE:

We are seeking a Senior Mechanical Engineer to join our Hardware Engineering team. This role has a strong mechanical engineering focus, with exposure to electrical systems primarily from an integration and interface perspective. The position supports new system development, payload integration, and subsystem design for uncrewed surface vessels, working closely with electrical and software engineers to ensure robust, manufacturable, and field-ready hardware solutions.

The ideal candidate is highly proficient in mechanical design and CAD, experienced in bringing complex mechanical systems from concept through production, and comfortable collaborating with electrical engineers to define interfaces, routing, and packaging—without being the primary electrical designer.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES AND REQUIREMENTS:

 - System Development & Architecture
   Lead and contribute to the mechanical design and integration of new systems and payloads, including structural components, enclosures, mounts, and overall system packaging. Support electrical integration by defining mechanical interfaces, routing paths, and mounting solutions.

 - Mechanical Design
   Create detailed CAD models, assemblies, and engineering drawings using SolidWorks for mechanical structures, housings, brackets, mounts, and mechanisms suitable for marine operating environments.

 - Electrical Interface Support
   Work alongside electrical engineers to support integration of wiring harnesses, connectors, and electronic subsystems. Interpret electrical schematics as needed and assist with mechanical aspects of cable routing, strain relief, connector mounting, and environmental protection.

 - Integration & Testing
   Participate in hardware builds, subsystem and bench testing, vehicle integration, and troubleshooting to validate mechanical designs and ensure reliable performance in operational conditions.

 - Cross-Disciplinary Collaboration
   Collaborate closely with mechanical, electrical, and software engineers to specify, design, build, test, and integrate mechanically driven subsystems and electromechanical components (e.g., actuators, motors, power distribution hardware) into vehicles.

 - Documentation
   Produce and maintain accurate mechanical documentation including CAD drawings, bills of materials, interface control documentation (ICDs), test results, and configuration management records.

 - Manufacturing & Production Support
   Partner with technicians and manufacturing teams to ensure designs are manufacturable, cost-effective, serviceable, and appropriate for rugged, marine environments.




QUALIFICATIONS:

 - 6+ years of experience in mechanical engineering design and system integration.

 - Strong proficiency in SolidWorks for mechanical CAD modeling and drafting.

 - Solid understanding of mechanical systems, materials, tolerances, and structural design.

 - Working familiarity with electrical systems sufficient to support integration (e.g., connectors, harness routing, grounding, environmental sealing).

 - Hands-on experience with prototyping, assembly, testing, and troubleshooting mechanical and electromechanical systems.

 - Experience working in cross-functional engineering teams and integrating subsystems into complete platforms.

 - Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering or a closely related field.

 - U.

source_jobs.title: Embedded Perception Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ABOUT THE ROLE:

As an Embedded Perception Engineer, you will own how our autonomous systems perceive and understand the world — in real time, in operational environments, and under demanding maritime conditions.

This role goes beyond model training. You will architect, deploy, and optimize perception pipelines that run reliably on embedded edge hardware. You’ll work across vision models, sensor fusion, and high-performance inference to deliver robust situational awareness to autonomous surface vessels executing real-world missions.

If you thrive in dynamic environments, enjoy squeezing maximum performance from embedded AI systems, and want to see your work field-tested and mission-proven, this role offers immediate and meaningful impact.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES AND REQUIREMENTS:


PERCEPTION SYSTEM OWNERSHIP

 - Own end-to-end perception pipelines deployed to operational systems

 - Deliver high-reliability solutions aligned with mission requirements


VISION MODEL DEVELOPMENT

 - Design, train, and optimize perception models for maritime environments

 - Develop object detection, classification, and tracking systems

 - Work with large vision models (ViT, CLIP, SAM, or similar)


SENSOR FUSION & INTEGRATION

 - Fuse multi-modal sensor data (camera, radar, lidar) into unified perception outputs

 - Collaborate with hardware teams to select and integrate sensors

 - Develop and validate low-level sensor drivers for high-integrity data pipelines


EMBEDDED DEPLOYMENT & OPTIMIZATION

 - Deploy models using NVIDIA Triton Inference Server

 - Optimize inference for latency and throughput on edge hardware

 - Apply quantization, pruning, and other optimization techniques


CROSS-TEAM COLLABORATION

 - Integrate perception outputs into autonomy stacks alongside mission software and platform teams

 - Support troubleshooting of perception performance in operational environments


FIELD PERFORMANCE & MONITORING

 - Define metrics and evaluation frameworks to monitor deployed system health

 - Investigate perception failures and drive resolution across teams


END-USER ENGAGEMENT

 - Partner with operators and customers to translate field challenges into engineering solutions




QUALIFICATIONS:

 - Bachelor’s or higher in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Robotics, Machine Learning, or related field

 - 3+ years developing and deploying computer vision or perception systems in C++ and/or Python on Linux

 - Experience with modern deep learning frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow)

 - Hands-on experience with large vision models (ViT, CLIP, SAM, etc.)

 - Experience implementing sensor fusion across heterogeneous modalities

 - Proficiency with NVIDIA Triton, TensorRT, ONNX Runtime, or equivalent inference tooling

 - Ability to quickly navigate and understand complex codebases

 - Strong systems-thinking mindset

 - U.S. Citizenship and eligibility for U.S. security clearance




PREFERRED SKILLS:

 - Experience deploying perception systems on embedded platforms (Jetson, FPGA, similar)

 - Familiarity with maritime or aerial sensor environments

 - Experience optimizing models for edge inference

 - Knowledge of autonomy frameworks (ROS2, MOOS-IvP)

 - Experience with safety-critical or real-time systems

 - Familiarity with military systems and defense acquisition processes




WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

You won’t

source_jobs.title: Robotics Engineer - Ground Autonomy
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Robotics Engineer – Ground Autonomy, you will design, build, and deploy autonomy systems for unmanned ground vehicles operating in complex, unstructured environments.

This role spans the full autonomy stack — from control systems and motion planning to perception integration and mission execution. You won’t just write algorithms; you’ll own systems that move real machines across real terrain under real operational constraints.

If you are energized by making intelligent systems move safely and confidently through the world, and want your work tested beyond the lab, this role offers immediate and meaningful impact.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


END-TO-END AUTONOMY OWNERSHIP

 - Own ground autonomy systems deployed to operational platforms

 - Deliver reliable, mission-ready autonomy software


CONTROL & MOTION PLANNING

 - Design and implement control algorithms (path following, trajectory generation, vehicle dynamics modeling)

 - Develop motion planning strategies for diverse and dynamic terrains

 - Optimize for stability, precision, and safety


PERCEPTION INTEGRATION

 - Develop and integrate perception pipelines for obstacle detection, terrain analysis, and environmental understanding

 - Fuse heterogeneous sensors (camera, lidar, radar, IMU) into robust autonomy outputs


AUTONOMY ARCHITECTURE

 - Build and extend ROS2-based autonomy stacks

 - Implement mission planning, behavior trees, and real-time navigation logic

 - Maintain modular, scalable autonomy architecture


CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COLLABORATION

 - Partner with embedded, hardware, and mission software teams

 - Drive autonomy integration from prototype to field deployment


FIELD TESTING & DEBUGGING

 - Develop monitoring and performance metrics for deployed systems

 - Diagnose autonomy failures in real-world environments

 - Support live testing, demonstrations, and deployments


CUSTOMER & OPERATOR ENGAGEMENT

 - Translate operational ground mission needs into autonomy capabilities

 - Balance field constraints with technical feasibility



QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree in Robotics, Computer Science, Electrical or Mechanical Engineering (or related field)

 - 3+ years of experience in C++ and/or Python in Linux environments

 - Hands-on experience deploying ROS2-based autonomy systems on real-world ground platforms

 - Experience designing control systems for autonomous vehicles (path planning, trajectory following, vehicle dynamics)

 - Experience building perception systems for ground autonomy

 - Strong systems-thinking and debugging skills

 - Passion for mission-critical real-world robotics

 - U.S. Citizenship and eligibility for U.S. security clearance
   
   


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience with multi-agent or coordinated ground vehicle systems

 - SLAM, GPS-denied navigation, or advanced localization techniques

 - Motion planning algorithms (RRT, MPC, A*, etc.)

 - Experience with heterogeneous sensor integration

 - Real-time or safety-critical systems experience

 - Familiarity with military ground systems and operational tactics
   
   


WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

You will build autonomy systems that operate beyond controlled environments — on terrain that challenges perception, control, and decision-making in real time. Your work will directly impact how autonomous ground platforms perform in opera

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Software Engineer - Distributed Systems
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a Senior Backend Engineer to drive the development of scalable, high-performance backend systems that power our autonomous platforms.

In this role, you will be hands-on in architecture and development as a senior individual contributor. You will be responsible for designing and building highly concurrent, distributed systems in Go, helping set technical direction, and ensuring reliable, secure, and performant backend services.

You will work closely with frontend, platform, autonomy, and embedded teams to deliver systems that operate reliably in real-world, mission-critical environments.

This role is ideal for someone who combines deep backend expertise with strong technical judgment and thrives in fast-paced, high-ownership environments.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


TECHNICAL OWNERSHIP

 - Drive execution and technical excellence across backend projects

 - Set engineering standards, conduct code reviews, and guide architectural decisions

 - Contribute to backend roadmap and delivery in partnership with product and engineering leadership

 - Foster a culture of ownership, quality, and continuous improvement


BACKEND ARCHITECTURE & DEVELOPMENT

 - Design, build, and maintain scalable, highly concurrent backend services in Go

 - Architect distributed systems that support high throughput, reliability, and real-time operations

 - Define service boundaries, APIs, and data models across systems

 - Design services for high availability and fault tolerance across availability zones

 - Evolve singleton services into multi-zone, distributed deployments


SYSTEM PERFORMANCE & RELIABILITY

 - Optimize backend systems for performance, scalability, and resilience

 - Identify and resolve bottlenecks in distributed systems and concurrency-heavy workloads

 - Ensure systems meet high standards for uptime and operational reliability

 - Improve observability, maintainability, and operational readiness of backend services


CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COLLABORATION

 - Partner with frontend, platform, autonomy, and embedded teams to integrate services across applications and devices

 - Support end-to-end system design from edge devices to cloud infrastructure

 - Contribute to system-level architecture across the stack

 - Help align backend capabilities with product, platform, and mission needs


SECURITY & BEST PRACTICES

 - Enforce secure development practices across backend systems

 - Maintain high standards for code quality, testing, documentation, and reliability

 - Support technical reviews, design discussions, and production readiness efforts

 - Build systems with security, scalability, and long-term maintainability in mind




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 6+ years of experience in backend engineering, distributed systems engineering, or related software engineering roles

 - Strong expertise in Go and building highly concurrent systems

 - Experience designing and operating highly available, fault-tolerant distributed systems at scale

 - Experience with consensus and coordination primitives, such as etcd, Raft, leader election, or similar patterns for multi-zone deployments

 - Strong API design and system architecture skills

 - Experience with Kubernetes and containerized environments

 - Deep understanding of performance optimization, data structures, and system design

 - Ability to drive proj

source_jobs.title: Senior Full Stack Integration Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ABOUT THE ROLE

HavocAI is seeking a Senior Full-Stack Integration Engineer to help lead a new team focused on building integrations above the vehicle platform layer. This team will connect C2-to-C2 systems, communications stacks, and other mission-critical software across commercial and customer-facing environments.

This senior role blends program-based integration work with core full-stack engineering responsibilities across backend services, APIs, SDKs, and customer- and developer-facing interfaces. You will operate across the full stack to deliver commercial-grade integration solutions, bringing both breadth and depth to systems integration.

As a senior engineer, you will help shape technical direction, mentor other team members, and own integrations end to end. You will also build, maintain, and grow HavocAI’s open-source SDKs, including engaging with and supporting the developer community that uses them.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in high-level object modeling, system interoperability, full-stack development, and translating complex requirements into reliable, production-ready integrations for commercial customers.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead the design, development, and maintenance of integrations between C2 systems, communications platforms, and related infrastructure

 - Build full-stack solutions spanning backend services, APIs, SDKs, and front-end interfaces that support commercial integration needs

 - Build and extend open-source SDKs, ensuring they are reliable, well-documented, and easy to use

 - Engage with and support the open-source developer community, fostering adoption, feedback, and contribution

 - Model and design high-level abstractions that enable interoperability across diverse systems

 - Collaborate with Product, Platform, Autonomy, Cloud, Commercial, and customer-facing teams to deliver integration solutions aligned with mission and customer needs

 - Translate complex customer, program, and technical requirements into scalable integration architectures

 - Mentor engineers and contribute to internal standards and best practices for integration architecture, full-stack development, and community-facing SDK development

 - Own integrations end to end, from technical discovery and architecture through implementation, validation, deployment, and support




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 7+ years of senior-level software engineering experience, with a track record of owning complex projects end to end

 - Strong programming experience in Go and Python

 - Full-stack development experience, including modern front-end frameworks such as React and TypeScript, alongside backend services

 - Demonstrated experience designing and implementing system integrations, including APIs, SDKs, communications interfaces, or distributed software systems

 - Ability to model high-level abstractions and design integration architectures across complex systems

 - Experience with version control, CI/CD, and containerized environments, including Git, Docker, and Kubernetes

 - Excellent communication skills and ability to collaborate across technical and non-technical stakeholders

 - Strong ownership, technical judgment, and ability to operate in ambiguity

 - Must be a U.S. Citizen and eligible to obtain a U.S. Government security clearance




PREFERRED SKILLS

 - Background in mission sy

source_jobs.title: Staff Cloud Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a Staff Cloud Platform Engineer to drive the design and operation of the cloud platform that powers our autonomous systems.

In this role, you will be a hands-on staff-level individual contributor, owning the design of secure, reliable, and cost-effective cloud infrastructure while helping set the technical direction for how we provision, deploy, and operate services.

You will work closely with backend, SRE, data, autonomy, and security teams to deliver platform capabilities that support mission-critical workloads in real-world environments.

This role is ideal for someone who combines deep cloud and infrastructure expertise with strong technical judgment, thrives in fast-paced, high-ownership environments, and is energized by complex and ambiguous platform challenges.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


TECHNICAL OWNERSHIP

 - Drive execution and technical excellence across cloud platform projects

 - Set infrastructure standards, conduct design and code reviews, and guide architectural decisions

 - Shape the cloud platform roadmap in partnership with product and engineering leadership

 - Foster a culture of ownership, quality, automation, and continuous improvement


CLOUD PLATFORM ARCHITECTURE & DEVELOPMENT

 - Design platform capabilities with reliability, scalability, security, and cost efficiency in mind


SYSTEM PERFORMANCE, RELIABILITY & COST

 - Establish reliability practices, including SLIs, SLOs, error budgets, and incident response

 - Improve observability, maintainability, and operational readiness across cloud platform services


CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COLLABORATION

 - Partner with backend, SRE, data, autonomy, and security teams to deliver platform capabilities across the stack

 - Support end-to-end system design from edge devices to cloud infrastructure

 - Coordinate across teams to build and maintain release processes and CI/CD pipelines

 - Help align platform capabilities with product, engineering, security, and mission needs


SECURITY & BEST PRACTICES

 - Enforce secure cloud practices, including IAM least privilege, secrets management, and network segmentation

 - Maintain high standards for automation, testing, observability, documentation, and infrastructure reliability

 - Support technical reviews, design discussions, and production readiness efforts

 - Build systems with security, scalability, and long-term maintainability in mind




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 10+ years of experience in cloud platform, infrastructure, DevOps, or related engineering roles

 - Deep expertise with AWS core services, including compute, networking, storage, IAM, and managed data services

 - Strong experience with Infrastructure as Code

 - Hands-on experience operating Kubernetes, including EKS, and containerized environments

 - Strong

source_jobs.title: Controller
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ROLE OVERVIEW

The Controller will serve as a senior leader within the Finance organization. This executive will be responsible for building, scaling, and leading a best-in-class accounting function capable of supporting a complex, high-growth business with rigorous financial reporting, audit, compliance, and internal control requirements.

This is a high-impact, high-visibility leadership role requiring deep technical accounting expertise, strong operational discipline, and the ability to lead through scale. The Controller will own the integrity of the company’s financial reporting, drive a timely and accurate close process, ensure audit readiness, strengthen internal controls, and partner cross-functionally with executive leadership to support strategic growth initiatives.

The ideal candidate has operated in a public-company environment or similarly rigorous reporting environment and brings a proven track record of scaling accounting organizations, implementing robust financial infrastructure, and leading teams through increasing operational complexity. This leader must be equally comfortable advising at the executive and Board level, developing scalable processes, and diving into the details when needed.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


FINANCIAL REPORTING & CLOSE

 - Lead the preparation of monthly, quarterly, and annual consolidated financial statements in accordance with U.S. GAAP.

 - Own the company’s close process, ensuring timely, accurate, and scalable financial reporting while continuously improving close timelines, process quality, and operational efficiency.

 - Oversee general ledger activity, account reconciliations, consolidation processes, and overall financial statement integrity.

 - Develop and deliver high-quality financial reporting packages for executive leadership, the Board stakeholders, and, as applicable, Audit Committee audiences.

 - Establish reporting standards, review mechanisms, and operating rhythms that support accuracy, accountability, and executive-level decision-making.




TECHNICAL ACCOUNTING

 - Serve as the company’s senior technical accounting leader, owning accounting policies, technical memos, and conclusions across complex areas including revenue recognition under ASC 606, inventory, leases under ASC 842, stock-based compensation, business combinations, and other areas as the company scales.

 - Monitor, assess, and lead implementation of new accounting pronouncements and evolving reporting requirements.

 - Advise the CFO and executive leadership on the accounting implications of strategic initiatives, transactions, customer contracts, financing activities, acquisitions, and new business models.

 - Partner with Legal, FP&A, Operations, Supply Chain, and external advisors to ensure accounting positions are well-documented, supportable, and aligned with company objectives.




EXTERNAL REPORTING & READINESS

 - Build financial reporting processes, documentation, and governance practices aligned with public-company standards and increasing external scrutiny.

 - Partner with external auditors, legal counsel, and other advisors on complex reporting, disclosure, and compliance matters.

 - Contribute to the company’s readiness for enhanced external reporting requirements, including preparation for future liquidity events, large-scale financing, public-company expectations,

source_jobs.title: Senior Frontend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a Senior Frontend Engineer to build and scale the user interfaces that power our platform.

In this role, you will develop high-performance, data-driven web applications, including map-based and visualization-heavy interfaces. You will work closely with backend, platform, product, and design teams to deliver reliable, scalable frontend systems that handle real-time data and complex workflows.

This role is ideal for someone who is deeply technical, performance-focused, and excited about building systems that operate in real-world, mission-critical environments.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


FRONTEND SYSTEMS DEVELOPMENT

 - Develop scalable, performant user interfaces using React

 - Implement complex workflows and UI components for data-rich applications

 - Build interfaces that support real-time data, operational decision-making, and mission-critical workflows

 - Deliver intuitive, reliable user experiences for complex technical systems


DATA INTEGRATION & PERFORMANCE

 - Integrate frontend systems with backend services using REST, GraphQL, or similar APIs

 - Optimize rendering performance for large datasets and real-time updates

 - Ensure responsive, reliable performance across devices, browsers, and operating environments

 - Support frontend performance monitoring, debugging, and continuous improvement


ARCHITECTURE & MAINTAINABILITY

 - Contribute to frontend architecture, including state management, data flow, and application structure

 - Build and maintain reusable component libraries and UI systems

 - Write clean, testable, maintainable, and well-documented code

 - Improve frontend reliability, scalability, and developer experience over time


CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COLLABORATION

 - Partner with backend, platform, product, and design teams to deliver end-to-end features

 - Collaborate with UX/design to ensure high-quality implementation and usability

 - Participate in code reviews, design discussions, technical planning, and roadmap execution

 - Help align frontend capabilities with product, platform, and mission needs




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5–8+ years of experience in frontend engineering or related software engineering roles

 - Strong expertise in React and modern JavaScript or TypeScript

 - Experience building map-based or geospatial applications using tools such as Mapbox, Leaflet, or Deck.gl http://Deck.gl"

 - Experience building data-driven, high-performance web applications

 - Experience working with APIs and asynchronous data flows

 - Strong understanding of frontend architecture, state management, and performance optimization

 - Experience with modern frontend tooling, build systems, and development workflows

 - Ability to build reliable interfaces for complex workflows and data-rich applications

 - Strong technical judgment and ability to operate independently in fast-moving environments

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills

 - U.S. citizenship and ability to obtain and maintain a security clearance




PREFERRED SKILLS



 - Familiarity with WebGL or advanced visualization frameworks

 - Experience with automated testing frameworks such as Jest, Cypress, or similar tools

 - Understanding of design systems and reusable component architecture

 - Experience with real-time systems, data streaming applications, or operational dashboards

source_jobs.title: Director of Navy Programs
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ROLE OVERVIEW

HavocAI is seeking a Director of Navy Programs to lead the identification, capture, and execution of strategic U.S. Navy and maritime defense opportunities. This role will be responsible for driving revenue growth, building and deepening customer relationships, shaping opportunities early, and positioning HavocAI as a leader in autonomous maritime operations.

The ideal candidate brings a strong understanding of Navy acquisition, operational priorities, and maritime mission needs, combined with proven business development, capture, and program execution experience. This individual will work closely with Growth, Product, Engineering, Mission Operations, and executive leadership to mature opportunities from early customer engagement through award and execution.

This is a high-impact role for someone who can operate strategically with senior stakeholders while also getting into the details required to shape solutions, support product direction, and win complex defense opportunities.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Identify, qualify, shape, and capture Navy and maritime defense opportunities aligned with HavocAI’s strategic priorities

 - Develop and execute account and capture strategies across key Navy customers, program offices, operational commands, and related defense organizations

 - Build and maintain trusted relationships with Navy stakeholders, primes, shipbuilders, defense partners, and industry leaders

 - Lead opportunity-shaping activities, including customer engagement, teaming strategy, competitive positioning, requirements influence, and win strategy development

 - Partner cross-functionally with Growth, Product, Engineering, Mission Operations, and leadership teams to align customer needs with HavocAI’s technical and operational capabilities

 - Support proposal development, pricing strategy, customer messaging, and overall pursuit execution

 - Maintain accurate pipeline forecasting, opportunity tracking, and customer engagement records within CRM systems

 - Represent HavocAI at industry events, exercises, demonstrations, customer meetings, and partner engagements

 - Help expand HavocAI’s presence across maritime autonomy, unmanned systems, distributed operations, and related mission areas

 - Translate customer needs and operational feedback into actionable product, engineering, and program priorities

 - Support the development of repeatable go-to-market strategies for Navy and maritime defense customers




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 7+ years of experience in defense business development, capture, program development, or Navy-focused sales

 - Strong understanding of U.S. Navy acquisition processes, organizations, funding pathways, and operational priorities

 - Demonstrated experience leading or supporting successful captures within the defense market

 - Existing relationships across Navy, DoD, maritime, shipbuilding, or defense industry stakeholders

 - Ability to operate effectively in fast-moving, highly collaborative environments

 - Strong communication, presentation, negotiation, and strategic thinking skills

 - Ability to engage at both the executive and operator level, translating mission needs into actionable business and product opportunities

 - Willingness and ability to get into the details of customer requirements, product capabilities, and technical solution developme

source_jobs.title: Facility Security Officer (FSO)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a Security Officer to help scale and manage our security program in support of U.S. government and defense contracts. This role will focus on personnel security, clearance management, visit coordination, physical security, and compliance support across a fast-moving defense technology environment.

This is a hands-on role for someone who understands government security requirements but is also comfortable operating in a high-growth technology company where speed, ownership, and sound judgment matter. You will work closely with People Operations, Legal, Contracts, Program Management, Engineering, and Business Development to ensure our teams can execute securely and efficiently.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Serve as the company’s Facility Security Officer (FSO) to maintain NISPOM compliance, DCSA requirements, self-inspections, audits, and corrective actions.

 - Administer and maintain the company's Facility Clearance (FCL), including all associated documentation, reporting, and compliance activities.

 - Manage personnel security workflows, including clearance initiation, reinvestigations, onboarding, offboarding, access tracking, and employee security support.

 - Maintain accurate clearance and access records across internal systems and government platforms.

 - Coordinate with government customers, prime contractors, subcontractors, and internal teams on personnel security and access matters.

 - Conduct employee security briefings, debriefings, annual refresher training, foreign travel reporting support, and insider threat awareness activities.

 - Help develop and improve scalable security processes, policies, playbooks, and training materials.

 - Partner with Recruiting and People Operations to ensure security requirements are integrated into hiring, onboarding, and employee lifecycle workflows.

 - Administer physical security controls for company facilities and restricted areas.

 - Assist in the management of access control systems, visitor management processes, badge issuance, and facility access procedures.

 - Support the design, implementation, and maintenance of secure workspaces, classified processing areas, and storage requirements as applicable.

 - Support a security culture that protects sensitive information while enabling teams to move quickly in support of the mission.

 - Handle sensitive personal, company, technical, and government information with discretion and professionalism.
   
   


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3–6 years of experience in security, personnel security, government contracting, defense security, or a related security operations role.

 - Experience supporting cleared personnel and managing security clearance workflows.

 - Familiarity with NISPOM, DCSA processes, DD254s, visit requests, personnel security, and classified contract requirements.

 - Working knowledge of DISS and related security systems.

 - Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple cases, deadlines, stakeholders, and shifting priorities.

 - Excellent communication skills and ability to support employees who may be new to government security requirements.

 - High attention to detail, strong follow-through, and sound judgment when handling sensitive information.

 - Ability to operate independently in a fast-paced, high-growth, mission-focused environm

source_jobs.title: Director of Autonomy
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ABOUT THE ROLE

As the Director of Autonomy, you will lead the technical direction, execution, and growth within HavocAI’s autonomy organization. You will be responsible for building and scaling the systems that enable autonomous vehicles to perceive, plan, coordinate, and operate in complex maritime environments.

This is a senior technical leadership role for someone who has deep experience across robotics, perception, motion planning, controls, and fielded autonomous systems. You will lead teams of engineers from a technical perspective, set architecture and execution priorities, mentor senior engineers, and ensure that autonomy capabilities move quickly from concept to deployed mission capability.

This role requires both breadth and depth. You should be able to reason across perception, planning, control, vehicle behavior, multi-agent coordination, simulation, testing, and deployment. You will work closely with engineering, product, field operations, hardware, mission software, and customer-facing teams to deliver robust autonomy systems that operate reliably in the real world.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


AUTONOMY TECHNICAL LEADERSHIP

 - Own the technical direction for HavocAI’s autonomy systems across perception, planning, controls, behaviors, and vehicle-level decision-making

 - Define autonomy architecture, interfaces, development standards, and integration strategies across multiple engineering teams

 - Lead technical tradeoffs across performance, safety, reliability, latency, compute constraints, scalability, and mission needs

 - Ensure autonomy capabilities are designed for real-world maritime operations, not just simulation or controlled environments

 - Drive technical alignment between autonomy, perception, mission software, embedded systems, hardware, and field operations




ROBOTICS, PLANNING & CONTROL SYSTEMS

 - Guide development of motion planning, path planning, behavior planning, control, and vehicle autonomy systems

 - Oversee autonomy capabilities for navigation, collision avoidance, target following, station keeping, formation behavior, and multi-vessel coordination

 - Ensure autonomy algorithms are robust to uncertain environments, degraded sensors, dynamic contacts, and complex maritime operating conditions

 - Partner with perception and sensor teams to ensure autonomy systems use perception outputs effectively and safely

 - Drive the maturation of autonomy from prototype capabilities into reliable, repeatable, fielded systems




TEAM LEADERSHIP & MENTORSHIP

 - Lead and mentor a high-performing team of autonomy, robotics, perception, planning, and controls engineers

 - Provide technical guidance to engineering managers, tech leads, and senior individual contributors

 - Establish strong engineering practices around design reviews, architecture reviews, testing, simulation, integration, and field readiness

 - Help recruit, evaluate, and develop top technical talent across the autonomy organization

 - Create a culture of ownership, technical rigor, speed, and mission focus




FIELDED AUTONOMY EXECUTION

 - Drive autonomy development from requirements through design, implementation, simulation, field test, deployment, and operational iteration

 - Ensure autonomy systems are testable, observable, debuggable, and measurable in real-world environments

 - Use field data, logs

source_jobs.title: Vehicle Autonomy Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ABOUT THE ROLE

As the Vehicle Autonomy Team Lead, you will lead the team responsible for building, integrating, and fielding the on-vehicle autonomy stack for HavocAI’s autonomous surface vessels. This team owns the autonomy capabilities that run onboard our vessels and enable them to safely navigate, execute missions, respond to dynamic maritime environments, and operate reliably in the field.

This is a senior technical leadership role for someone with deep experience in robotics, autonomy software, motion planning, controls, vehicle behavior, and real-world system integration. You will lead a team of engineers from a technical perspective, set engineering priorities, guide architecture decisions, mentor teammates, and ensure that autonomy capabilities are delivered with speed, rigor, and operational reliability.

This role is hands-on and field-oriented. You should be comfortable moving between system design, code review, debugging logs, evaluating autonomy performance, supporting field tests, and working cross-functionally with perception, mission software, embedded systems, hardware, and operations teams.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


VEHICLE AUTONOMY LEADERSHIP

 - Lead the technical execution of the on-vehicle autonomy team

 - Own autonomy capabilities that run onboard autonomous surface vessels, including navigation, planning, control, behaviors, safety logic, and autonomy execution

 - Define technical priorities, architecture, interfaces, and development standards for vehicle autonomy systems

 - Guide autonomy design decisions across reliability, latency, safety, compute constraints, mission performance, and field maintainability

 - Ensure the vehicle autonomy stack is robust, observable, testable, and ready for real-world maritime operations




TEAM LEADERSHIP & TECHNICAL MENTORSHIP

 - Lead, mentor, and support a team of autonomy, robotics, planning, and controls engineers

 - Provide technical guidance through design reviews, architecture reviews, code reviews, test planning, and field-readiness reviews

 - Help engineers break down complex autonomy problems into executable technical work

 - Establish strong engineering practices for autonomy development, integration, simulation, testing, and deployment

 - Create a team culture built on ownership, technical excellence, mission focus, and fast iteration




INTEGRATION, TESTING & FIELD DEPLOYMENT

 - Drive autonomy capabilities from concept through implementation, simulation, hardware-in-the-loop testing, field testing, and deployment

 - Support vessel integration, field tests, customer demonstrations, and operational evaluations

 - Diagnose and resolve autonomy issues using logs, telemetry, vehicle data, simulation results, and field observations

 - Build feedback loops between field failures, autonomy improvements, and product roadmap decisions

 - Ensure the team delivers autonomy software that can be operated, debugged, and maintained in real mission environments




CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COLLABORATION

 - Work closely with perception, mission software, embedded systems, hardware, product, program, and field operations teams

 - Translate mission needs and product requirements into vehicle autonomy capabilities

 - Coordinate interfaces between onboard autonomy, mission planning, command and control, sensors, and platform systems

 - Communic

source_jobs.title: Contracts Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ABOUT THE ROLE

The Contracts Manager is responsible for leading the full lifecycle of government and commercial contracts, supporting the company’s growth from capture and proposal through contract execution and closeout.

This role will negotiate, administer, and manage prime contracts, subcontracts, teaming agreements, non-disclosure agreements, and other commercial agreements while ensuring compliance with applicable FAR, DFARS, and company policies.

Partnering closely with Business Development, Program Management, Finance, Engineering, Supply Chain, and Legal, the Contracts Manager will identify and mitigate contractual risk, support proposal development, negotiate terms and conditions, and provide guidance on contractual obligations throughout program execution.

The ideal candidate is an experienced government contracts professional who thrives in a fast-paced, high-growth defense technology environment. They are comfortable balancing risk with business objectives, developing scalable contracting processes, and supporting both strategic pursuits and active programs.

Success in this role requires strong negotiation skills, sound business judgment, exceptional attention to detail, and the ability to work collaboratively across technical and business teams.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Manage the full contract lifecycle from proposal support through award, execution, modification, and closeout.

 - Draft, review, negotiate, and administer government and commercial contracts, subcontracts, teaming agreements, NDAs, and other contractual documents.

 - Interpret and ensure compliance with FAR, DFARS, and customer-specific contractual requirements.

 - Partner with Business Development and Capture teams to review solicitations, identify contractual risks, and support proposal submissions.

 - Advise Program Managers and functional leaders on contractual obligations, deliverables, changes, and compliance requirements.

 - Coordinate with Legal, Finance, Supply Chain, and Engineering to resolve contractual issues and negotiate favorable business terms.

 - Maintain contract records, reporting, and internal controls to support audits and regulatory compliance.

 - Develop and improve scalable contract management processes to support company growth.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Public Administration, Law, or a related field.

 - 7+ years of experience managing government contracts within the defense, aerospace, or federal technology sectors.

 - Strong working knowledge of FAR, DFARS, and common U.S. Government contract types, including FFP, CPFF, T&M, and IDIQ contracts.

 - Experience supporting proposal development, contract negotiations, and program execution.

 - Excellent negotiation, communication, analytical, and organizational skills.

 - Ability to manage multiple priorities in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.

 - Experience with OTAs, SBIR/STTR programs, export controls, including ITAR/EAR, and DCAA-compliant environments is preferred.

 - NCMA certification, such as CPCM, CFCM, or CCCM, is a plus.


BENEFITS:

 - 100% Employer paid Health, Dental and Vision Insurance for you and your families

 - Life Insurance (Employer Paid)

 - Ability to participate in the companies 401k program (Matching)

 - Unlimited PTO policy with an enforced 2 week minimum

 - Equity Pac

ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ABOUT THE ROLE:

HavocAI is seeking an Embedded Software Engineer to lead hardware/software development of embedded systems for our fleets of uncrewed surface vessels (USVs). This role will be pivotal in advancing the reliability and functionality of our uncrewed surface vessels, enabling groundbreaking applications in defense, marine research, and environmental monitoring. In this role, you will collaborate on projects ranging from prototyping and testing of new USV platforms to leveraging existing capabilities to form distributed sensing networks.

 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Lead the development and maintenance of robust low-level software control systems for uncrewed surface vessels (USVs).

 - Design, implement, and optimize drivers for various communication protocols such as CAN (J1939, NMEA2000, DroneCAN) and RS485 (ModBus).

 - Integrate and test drivers for new subsystems, including motors, batteries, actuators, and sensors, by working with vendors or vendor-provided datasheets.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams (hardware and autonomy engineers) to deliver software that abstracts hardware functions for both existing and new vehicle types.

 - Set up, exercise, document, and maintain hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) test environments to enable robust system testing.

 - Create and maintain software documentation, test schematics, and design notes for development projects.

 


QUALIFICATIONS:

 - Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, or similar engineering discipline

 - Proven experience developing, testing, and debugging embedded software for STM32 platforms using C/C++ with RTOS (e.g., ChibiOS, FreeRTOS).

 - In-depth knowledge of communication protocols, including CAN and RS485; experience using bus analysis tools to measure performance and troubleshoot

 - Experience working in a professional setting in a collaborative software development environment, using tools such as git, docker, Confluence, Jira, etc.

 - Strong foundational electronics skills, including soldering, prototyping, debugging, and schematic interpretation.

 - Ability to independently manage and execute prototype development projects.

 - Must be a US Citizen and be eligible to obtain a Secret Clearance

 


PREFERRED SKILLS:

 - Experience contributing to or extending PX4 or ArduPilot open-source autopilot codebases.

 - Background in automotive or marine systems with distributed, bus-based architectures.

 - Familiarity with implementing pub/sub architectures (e.g., DDS) on resource-constrained systems.

 - Familiarity with basic mechanical assembly and use of standard shop tools.

 - Experience with environmental or mission-critical applications of robotics.

 


LOCATION:

 - Must be based in the greater Boston area, with the ability to work onsite as needed.

 


BENEFITS:

 - 100% Employer paid Health, Dental and Vision Insurance for you and your families

 - Life Insurance (Employer Paid)

 - Ability to participate in the companies 401k program (Matching)

 - Unlimited PTO policy with an enforced 2 week minimum

 - Equity Package

 - Work / Home Office Stipend

 - Global Entry

 - 16 Week Paid Parental Leave

 - Monthly Health and Wellness Stipend



OUR VALUES:

 - Innovation: We are driven to break new ground. Every day presents an opportunity to challenge the status quo, think boldly, and

source_jobs.title: Technical Program Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Technical Program Manager, you will drive technical execution across complex robotics, autonomy, software, and defense programs. You will work closely with engineering, product, operations, field teams, and customers to turn mission needs into executable plans and delivered capabilities.

This role requires technical depth, strong program leadership, and the ability to operate across hardware, software, autonomy, systems integration, testing, and deployment. You will own schedules, milestones, risks, dependencies, and delivery plans while working directly with technical teams to keep execution moving.

This is not a traditional project coordination role. You will be expected to understand the systems being built, identify technical and programmatic risks, unblock engineering teams, communicate clearly with stakeholders, and help deliver mission-ready robotic systems at speed.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


PROGRAM EXECUTION & CROSS-FUNCTIONAL DELIVERY

 - Own execution for robotics, autonomy, software, and defense-related engineering programs

 - Drive planning, milestones, schedules, risks, dependencies, and cross-functional delivery

 - Translate customer needs, contract requirements, and mission objectives into clear technical plans

 - Coordinate across software, autonomy, hardware, manufacturing, field operations, product, and customer-facing teams

 - Identify risks early and work with engineering and company leadership to resolve them


ENGINEERING INTEGRATION & TEST

 - Partner with engineering leads to define requirements, interfaces, integration plans, test plans, and release criteria

 - Coordinate integration across software, hardware, autonomy, sensing, communications, and platform systems

 - Plan and support system testing, hardware-in-the-loop testing, field testing, demonstrations, and operational evaluations

 - Ensure teams are prepared with test objectives, success criteria, readiness reviews, data collection plans, and post-test analysis workflows

 - Track field findings, failure modes, corrective actions, and engineering closure


CUSTOMER, DEFENSE & TEAM OPERATIONS

 - Support execution of government, defense, and dual-use technology programs

 - Interface with defense, government, and commercial stakeholders as needed

 - Support proposal planning, customer briefings, program reviews, deliverables, and execution reporting

 - Communicate program status, risks, milestones, tradeoffs, and decisions clearly

 - Establish effective planning, documentation, reporting, escalation, and decision-making cadences

 - Help engineering teams move quickly while maintaining technical rigor, accountability, and execution discipline




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Robotics, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Systems Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, or a related technical field

 - 5+ years of experience in technical program management, engineering program leadership, systems engineering, robotics, defense technology, autonomy, software, or related fields

 - Experience managing complex technical programs with cross-functional engineering teams

 - Technical background in robotics, software systems, autonomy, hardware/software integration, defense systems, or fielded technology products

 - Ability to understand and communicate

source_jobs.title: Engineering Field Service Technician
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking an Engineering Field Service Technician to support the deployment, maintenance, and sustainment of our uncrewed surface vessel fleet. This role is highly hands-on and requires strong technical troubleshooting skills across mechanical, electrical, communications, and systems domains.

You will be responsible for diagnosing and repairing issues, replacing components, documenting changes, and ensuring vehicle readiness during testing, demonstrations, and customer deployments. This position is based out of our San Diego, CA office and requires extensive travel, primarily across the U.S., with international travel as needed.

When not traveling, this role will be onsite supporting integration, testing, maintenance, and vehicle readiness.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


FIELD SUPPORT

 - Travel to customer sites, test ranges, and demonstration events to provide hands-on maintenance, troubleshooting, and repair of USVs and related subsystems

 - Support field operations in demanding maritime, test, and customer-facing environments

 - Represent HavocAI professionally while working directly with customers, partners, and internal teams


MAINTENANCE & REPAIRS

 - Perform mechanical and electrical system maintenance, component replacement, and corrective actions to keep vehicles mission-ready

 - Diagnose and troubleshoot electrical, mechanical, communications, and system-level issues in the field

 - Work with engineering teams to implement temporary field fixes and long-term corrective actions

 - Install new subsystems, hardware modifications, and software updates on deployed vehicles


TESTING & VALIDATION

 - Support sea trials, system tests, demonstrations, and operational evaluations

 - Validate vehicle and subsystem performance against engineering specifications

 - Document test results, field findings, failures, and corrective actions

 - Provide clear feedback to engineering teams to improve reliability, maintainability, and performance


LOGISTICS, READINESS & DOCUMENTATION

 - Prepare equipment, spare parts, tools, and service kits for deployment

 - Maintain accurate service records, inventory, maintenance logs, and field activity reports

 - Create, update, and maintain user manuals, service guides, and maintenance documentation

 - Ensure maintenance actions, repairs, and system updates are clearly documented for traceability and continuous improvement




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of experience in field service, maintenance, technical support, or related hands-on technical roles, preferably involving complex electromechanical systems

 - Experience troubleshooting and maintaining electrical and communication systems

 - Comfortable working in maritime environments

 - Hands-on skills in mechanical assembly, wiring, system integration, and component-level troubleshooting

 - Strong troubleshooting skills, particularly across electrical and electromechanical systems

 - Familiarity with field service tools, test equipment, and safety procedures, including oscilloscopes, multimeters, hi-pot testers, serial communications analyzers, and related diagnostic equipment

 - Ability to read and interpret technical drawings, wiring diagrams, PCB schematics, and maintenance manuals

 - Ability to learn and implement basic software-based maintenance and troubleshooting programs, scripts,

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer - Business Analytics
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a Junior Software Engineer, Business Analytics to help build lightweight software tools, dashboards, automations, and data workflows that improve how HavocAI operates. This role sits at the intersection of software engineering, business operations, analytics, and rapid prototyping.

You will work with teams across the company to understand business needs, identify process gaps, and build practical tools that make information easier to access, analyze, and act on. This may include internal dashboards, reporting workflows, data pipelines, lightweight applications, AI-assisted tools, and automations that reduce manual work.

This is a great role for an early-career engineer who enjoys “vibe coding,” rapid iteration, AI-assisted development, and solving ambiguous business problems with simple, useful software. Success in this role requires curiosity, strong technical fundamentals, good judgment, and the ability to turn unclear requests into working tools quickly.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


BUSINESS ANALYTICS & INTERNAL TOOLS

 - Build dashboards, reports, and lightweight internal applications to support business decision-making

 - Work with teams across Engineering, Operations, Finance, People, GTM, and Program Management to understand workflow and reporting needs

 - Translate business questions into data models, queries, dashboards, and actionable insights

 - Develop tools that improve visibility into company operations, program execution, recruiting, finance, and team performance

 - Help standardize reporting workflows and reduce manual spreadsheet-based processes


SOFTWARE DEVELOPMENT & AUTOMATION

 - Build and maintain lightweight software tools, scripts, integrations, and automations

 - Use modern AI-assisted development tools to rapidly prototype and iterate on internal solutions

 - Connect systems through APIs, databases, spreadsheets, and workflow automation tools

 - Develop simple front-end interfaces, data visualizations, and back-end services as needed

 - Improve existing workflows by automating repetitive tasks and simplifying access to key information


DATA & SYSTEMS INTEGRATION

 - Pull, clean, organize, and transform data from internal systems and business tools

 - Support data quality, consistency, and documentation across business-critical workflows

 - Build integrations between tools such as HRIS, ATS, finance systems, CRM, project management software, and internal databases

 - Partner with stakeholders to validate data accuracy and ensure tools are reliable and easy to use

 - Help create repeatable processes for analytics, reporting, and operational tracking


COLLABORATION & PROBLEM SOLVING

 - Work closely with business and technical teams to understand needs, gather feedback, and improve solutions

 - Communicate clearly with non-technical stakeholders and translate requirements into practical technical outcomes

 - Move quickly from concept to prototype while maintaining good engineering practices

 - Document tools, workflows, assumptions, and handoff instructions

 - Support a culture of practical, high-ownership problem solving




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Information Systems, Business Analytics, or a related technical field, or equivalent practical experience

 - 1–3 years of experien

source_jobs.title: DevOps Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Havoc is a leader in all-domain collaborative autonomy. Its software-defined hardware approach powers military and commercial-grade autonomous systems across sea, air, and land to sense, decide, and act together in complex and contested environments. Havoc connects assets, enabling them to share information, adapt in real time, and continue operating even when communications are disrupted or denied. Havoc optimizes mission performance and minimizes human risk.

Havoc was founded in 2024 and headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island. Learn more at Havoc: All-Domain Collaborative Autonomy http://havocai.com/ .


ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a DevOps Engineer to join our Cloud Platform team and help build the infrastructure and tooling that powers our engineering organization.

In this role, you will focus on automation, CI/CD, and cloud infrastructure, enabling teams to build, deploy, and operate services reliably and efficiently. You will play a key role in creating paved paths and self-service tooling that improve developer velocity while maintaining high standards for reliability, security, and performance.

You will work closely with the Cloud Platform, SRE, and Data Engineering teams in a fast-paced environment where systems are built and tested in real-world conditions.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


AUTOMATION & PLATFORM ENABLEMENT

 - Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines that support the full software development lifecycle

 - Develop self-service tooling and standardized workflows for deployment and operations

 - Improve developer experience through automation, documentation, and engineering best practices

 - Help establish scalable platform capabilities that accelerate engineering teams


CLOUD INFRASTRUCTURE & OPERATIONS

 - Implement and manage cloud infrastructure using Infrastructure as Code

 - Deploy and support containerized workloads using Docker and Kubernetes

 - Maintain and improve development, staging, and production environments

 - Partner with Cloud Platform and SRE teams to ensure systems are scalable, reliable, secure, and cost-efficient


RELIABILITY, OBSERVABILITY & SECURITY

 - Implement observability solutions across logging, metrics, tracing, and alerting

 - Participate in incident response, root cause analysis, and continuous improvement efforts

 - Implement secure CI/CD pipelines, secrets management, and access controls

 - Contribute to operational readiness, reliability practices, and infrastructure resilience


CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COLLABORATION

 - Partner with engineering teams to support deployment, infrastructure, and operational needs

 - Troubleshoot infrastructure, deployment, and runtime issues across environments

 - Contribute to platform standards, technical documentation, and engineering best practices

 - Help improve engineering workflows through automation and collaboration




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3+ years of experience in DevOps, infrastructure, cloud platform, or related engineering roles

 - Strong experience with CI/CD systems and deployment automation

 - Hands-on experience with Docker and Kubernetes

 - Experience managing cloud infrastructure, preferably AWS

 - Familiarity with Infrastructure as Code tools such as Terraform or CloudFormation

 - Experience operating production systems with monitoring, logging, and alerting

 - Proficiency in scripting or programming languages such as Python, Bash, or Go

 - Solid understanding of Linux systems and networking fundamentals

 - Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work in a fast-paced environment

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills

 - U.S. citizenship and ability to obtain and maintain a U.S. Government security clearance if required




PREFERRED SKILLS

 - Experience working on a cloud platform or internal developer platform

 - Familiarity with Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) practices, including SLIs, SLOs, and error budgets

 - Experience supporting data pipelines or analytics infras

notes: Highly technical roles, strong focus on autonomy and AI. Recent founding and rapid hiring suggest a startup environment.
